title: Fix the Broken Unit Test
description: A refactor changed a list return value into a generator, breaking tests that call len().
pr_diff:
PR Diff:
  - def get_pending_jobs(queue):
  -     return [job for job in queue if job.status == "pending"]
  + def get_pending_jobs(queue):
  +     return (job for job in queue if job.status == "pending")
initial_ci_output:
FAILED tests/test_queue.py::test_pending_count
TypeError: object of type 'generator' has no len()

tests/test_queue.py:34: in test_pending_count
    assert len(get_pending_jobs(mock_queue)) == 3

FAILED tests/test_queue.py::test_pending_jobs_returns_iterable
TypeError: cannot pickle 'generator' object — downstream serialization requires list

files.tests/test_queue.py:
from src.queue import get_pending_jobs, Job


def make_queue():
    return [
        Job(job_id="j1", status="pending", priority=1),
        Job(job_id="j2", status="running", priority=2),
        Job(job_id="j3", status="pending", priority=0),
        Job(job_id="j4", status="pending", priority=3),
    ]


def test_pending_count():
    assert len(get_pending_jobs(make_queue())) == 3


def test_pending_jobs_are_serializable():
    import pickle
    result = get_pending_jobs(make_queue())
    pickle.dumps(result)
